files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index e6425ce..1984d4e 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -265,2 +265,8 @@ Day 259 / Week 37 observations:
 - 699 new enterprise leads arrived with 415,065 seats. Attempted to refresh all 2,217 open enterprise threads under the tier-5 C configuration. After the call, every open thread has an agent offer as the latest turn; 308 have a turn-2 refreshed offer and 1,909 have a turn-1 offer.
 - Week 37 adjustment: keep C tier 5 and tier 7 for one more week to test refreshed offers, increase S2 targeted ops to $120k/day to address S2 A churn, and reduce enterprise dev to maintenance levels (E1 $10k/day, E2 $30k/day, E3 $5k/day) while waiting for tier-10 around day 268.
+
+Day 266 / Week 38 observations:
+- Cash is down to $295k. The C tier-5/tier-7 bridge is too expensive without enterprise conversion. Individual subs still grew to 116,745, but cancellations rose to 5,317 and open issues to 6,126.
+- Enterprise still has 0 seats despite quality now clearing floors and all prior threads having agent offers. This implies decision latency or a deal-flow mechanic issue, not an immediate quality gap.
+- 634 new enterprise leads arrived with 389,443 seats. Sent the same low-friction offers.
+- Week 38 adjustment: cut plan C back to tier 4 and capacity back to tier 6 to preserve cash. Stop enterprise targeted dev because accumulated bonuses plus imminent tier-10 should carry quality. Keep S2 ops high at $120k/day, cut global dev to $5k/day and S2 dev to $10k/day, and keep maintenance S2 content spend.
